summary: McKenzie shares his experience receiving a SwiftCD physical copy of his Bingo Card Creator software, noting the impressive print quality. He documents the shipping timeline from order to delivery in Japan, marveling at the speed and low cost of international shipping for his small software business's physical product fulfillment.
key_points: Physical product fulfillment through SwiftCD produced professional-looking results even for a tiny software business
International shipping from the US to rural Japan took about a week at minimal cost (84 cents)
Even small details like print clarity on the CD contributed to a professional appearance for his product
money_quotes: What an age to be living in where you can get a package to a little ricefield on the other side of the world in about a week for 84 cents.
The printing is astonishingly clear (even the teeny-tiny text on the CD is easily legible) and in person it looks very professionally done.
Did I mention I live in Japan? Phones here can practically perform brain surgery.
key_insight: Modern fulfillment services let even the smallest software businesses deliver professional physical products globally at negligible cost.
